channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda a Global Health Emergency, Sparking Political Reactions
item.description: The World Health Organization has declared the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern. The announcement triggered widespre...

item.title: Bulgarian Singer DARA Wins Eurovision 2026 with Hit Song Bangaranga
item.description: DARA, born Darina Yotova in Varna, Bulgaria, has become the first Bulgarian artist to win the Eurovision Song Contest, taking the crown in 2026 with her vibrant and infectious track Bangaranga. At 27,...

item.title: Oburu Oginga Urges Party Discipline and Affirms ODM’s Role Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: On Sunday, May 17, 2026, Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) elder statesman Oburu Oginga addressed concerns about internal party discipline and leadership, particularly responding to criticism from Lind...

item.title: Bengal BJP Chief Sarcastically Invites Abhishek Banerjee to Falta Ahead of Repoll
item.description: In West Bengal, BJP state president Samik Bhattacharya issued a sarcastic invitation to TMC leader Abhishek Banerjee to campaign in the Falta constituency before the repoll scheduled on May 21, 2026. ...

item.title: Weekly Curated Visual Culture Highlights: Art, Design, and Architecture Stories from May 11-17, 2026
item.description: This week's edition of Fog Signals from Moss & Fog presents a captivating collection of visual stories that explore creativity across multiple disciplines. The roundup features hypnotic kinetic typogr...

item.title: Pakistan affirms PCA ruling limits India’s control on western rivers under Indus Waters Treaty
item.description: Pakistan has expressed strong satisfaction over a supplemental award issued by the Permanent Court of Arbitration (PCA) regarding the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT), which it says confirms Islamabad’s posi...

item.title: Republican Sen. Thom Tillis criticizes Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth over plans to downgrade U.S. Army Europe-Africa command and sideline Gen. Chris Donahue
item.description: Sen. Thom Tillis (R-N.C.) publicly criticized Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth in a detailed post on X, describing recent Pentagon decisions as 'amateur hour at best and deadly at worst.' Tillis specifi...

item.title: Israeli Embassy Holds Dialogue with Siargao Community Over Tourist Conduct
item.description: In response to growing concerns about the behavior of tourists in Siargao, the Israeli Embassy has initiated engagement with the local community to foster understanding and cooperation. Ambassador Dan...

item.title: Cyd Charisse: Iconic MGM Dancer and Leading Lady of the 1950s
item.description: Cyd Charisse (1922–2008) was a defining figure of the 1950s Hollywood musical, particularly under the MGM studio. Renowned for her remarkable athleticism, elegance, and famously long legs, Charisse tr...

item.title: Highlights from Negi’s Appearance on “Kapamilya Deal or No Deal”
item.description: Negi recently took on the role of the lucky star player in the popular Philippine game show “Kapamilya Deal or No Deal,” hosted by Luis Manzano. During her stint, she brought a fresh and entertaining ...

item.title: Majority of Australians Across Generations Expect to Be Worse Off After Budget
item.description: A recent Newspoll has revealed widespread dissatisfaction with the Federal Budget, with Australians across all age groups expecting to be financially worse off. The poll shows Labor’s primary vote ste...

item.title: Michael Carrick set to clarify Manchester United managerial future after victory over Nottingham Forest
item.description: Michael Carrick is anticipating clarity on his future as Manchester United manager within the coming days. This statement followed United’s 3-2 victory against Nottingham Forest, a game in which Bruno...

item.title: Why Concert Ticket Sales Are Dropping in 2026
item.description: Following the post-pandemic surge in concert attendance, the music industry is now facing a phenomenon called 'blue dot fever,' named after unsold seats on Ticketmaster seating charts. After COVID-19 ...

item.title: Zilhaj Moon Sighting Confirms Eidul Azha on May 27 in Pakistan
item.description: The Ruet-i-Hilal Committee has officially announced that the Zilhaj moon has been sighted, confirming that Eidul Azha will be celebrated on Wednesday, May 27, 2026. The committee held its main meeting...

item.title: Shashi Tharoor humorously defends idli against rasgulla comparison
item.description: In a recent viral exchange on social media, Congress MP Shashi Tharoor responded with his signature wit to a post comparing the South Indian delicacy idli with the Bengali sweet rasgulla. The original...

item.title: Automakers Shift Jobs to Focus on AI Expertise Amid Workforce Changes
item.description: The automotive industry is experiencing a significant shift driven by artificial intelligence. Companies like General Motors have laid off a portion of their IT workforce—over 600 salaried employees—a...

item.title: Alberta lawyer maintains independence campaign despite court ruling
item.description: A recent Alberta court ruling has struck down a citizen-initiated petition for an independence referendum, citing a breach in the duty to consult with First Nations. Justice Shaina Leonard of the Albe...

item.title: Shohei Ohtani drives in five runs as Dodgers defeat Angels decisively
item.description: Shohei Ohtani delivered a standout performance as the Los Angeles Dodgers routed the Los Angeles Angels, recording a season-high five RBIs. The two-way star, known for both his pitching and hitting, c...

item.title: Virat Kohli open to 2027 ODI World Cup if he can meaningfully contribute
item.description: India cricket legend Virat Kohli, now 37, has indicated that he will consider playing in the 2027 ODI World Cup only if he believes he can meaningfully contribute to the team. Kohli, who retired from ...

item.title: Three Killed and Several Injured in Oakland Pedestrian Crash
item.description: Three people were killed and several others injured late Saturday night when a speeding vehicle struck pedestrians at the intersection of 85th Avenue and International Boulevard in Oakland. The crash ...
